item.title: Learn How to Grow Evergreen Clematis
item.description:
Evergreen clematis are species that keep their green leaves through the cold months. These woody, climbing vines produce masses of fragrant, white or cream-colored flowers in winter or early spring, ideal for trellises, arbors, and pergolas in mild climates. Learn how to grow evergreen clematis in this guide. Read more.

The post Learn How to Grow Evergreen Clematis appeared first on Gardener's Path.
item.source:markdown:
Evergreen clematis are species that keep their green leaves through the cold months. These woody, climbing vines produce masses of fragrant, white or cream-colored flowers in winter or early spring, ideal for trellises, arbors, and pergolas in mild climates. Learn how to grow evergreen clematis in this guide. Read more.
The post [Learn How to Grow Evergreen Clematis](https://gardenerspath.com/plants/vines/grow-evergreen-clematis/) appeared first on [Gardener's Path](https://gardenerspath.com).

item.title: How to Grow and Care for Tiger Lilies
item.description:
Tiger lilies produce bold orange flowers with dark spots and recurved petals from mid to late summer. These Asian natives grow two to five feet tall with lance-shaped leaves. Hardy in Zones 4 to 8, they thrive in full sun to part shade with well-drained soil. Learn how to grow tiger lilies in this guide. Read more now.

The post How to Grow and Care for Tiger Lilies appeared first on Gardener's Path.

item.title: Growing an avocado grove under shade cloth in California’s Central Valley, with Tarcisio Ruiz
item.description:
“Avocado trees are like Baby Bear,” says University of California farm advisor Ben Faber. item.title: How to Grow and Care for Ageratum (Floss Flowers)
item.description:
Ageratum, or floss flowers, are versatile annuals with a long season that adds color to beds, borders, and containers – and perfect for cutting as well. Cut regularly, they produce a steady supply of adorable, densely clustered moptop flowers in tones of mauve or blue. Learn how to grow ageratum in this guide. Read more.

The post How to Grow and Care for Ageratum (Floss Flowers) appeared first on Gardener's Path.

item.title: Is Olive Oil Healthy for You? Oh So Good California EVOO
item.description:

item.title: Learn How to Grow Vibrant Diascia (Twinspur)
item.description:
Diascia produces clouds of colorful blooms from spring through fall in shades of pink, coral, apricot, and red. These compact trailing annuals grow six to 18 inches tall with a spreading habit ideal for containers, hanging baskets, and border fronts. Learn how to plant and grow twinspur in this guide. Read more now.

item.title: How to Grow Chinese Lantern in Containers
item.description:
Chinese lantern aka bladder cherry is an eye-catching perennial with papery orange husks that resemble glowing lanterns in late summer and fall. The plant spreads aggressively through underground rhizomes, which is why many gardeners grow it in containers. Learn how to grow Chinese lantern in containers in this guide.

item.title: How to Grow and Care for Flowering Maple (Abutilon)
item.description:
Flowering maples (Abutilon spp.) are tropical shrubs in the mallow family, grown for their pendulous, bell-shaped flowers in yellow, orange, red, pink, and white. Hardy outdoors in Zones 8 to 10, they also grow well as houseplants and container plants in colder climates. Learn how to grow flowering maples in this guide.

The post How to Grow and Care for Flowering Maple (Abutilon) appeared first on Gardener's Path.
